name: Transnevada and Encinar de Abrucena Loop
name_original: Peña Horadada y La Campana — circular desde Abrucena — Parque Nacional de Sierra Nevada
description: This difficult 25.8-mile mountain biking loop in Sierra Nevada National Park offers challenging terrain and stunning alpine views.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Abrucena offers diverse terrain within the Sierra Nevada Natural Park, characterized by its mountainous landscape. The region features significant peaks, including Cerro del Rayo (2,405 meters) and Buitre peak (2,465 meters), providing challenging ascents and descents. Riders can explore varied ecosystems, from pine slopes and ravines to rocky and arid sections, with some trails incorporating natural rock gardens and berms. This area is known for its high ecological value and extensive network of mountain bike trails. Best mountain bike trails around Abrucena The most popular mountain bike trail is Transnevada and Encinar de Abrucena Loop, a 25.8 miles (41.5 km) trail that takes 4 hours 8 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation changes through the mountainous landscape. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Abrucena Holm Oak Forest Trail, a moderate 15.3 miles (24.7 km) path. This route winds through varied terrain, including sections of holm oak forest and open landscapes. Local mountain bikers also love the Abrucena – Abrucena Cemetery loop from Abrucena, a 25.6 miles (41.2 km) trail leading through the local countryside, often completed in about 4 hours 26 minutes. Mountain biking around Abrucena is defined by mountainous terrain, pine slopes, and rocky trails. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult routes. The routes in Abrucena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 120 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Abrucena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there around Abrucena?
A: Abrucena offers a network of over 20 mountain bike trails. These routes traverse the diverse terrain of the Sierra Nevada Natural Park, providing options for various skill levels.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for mountain biking in Abrucena?
A: The trails around Abrucena range from moderate to difficult. You'll find 10 moderate routes and 12 difficult routes, featuring significant elevation changes, rocky sections, and varied terrain suitable for experienced riders seeking a challenge.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Abrucena?
A: While many trails in Abrucena are rated moderate to difficult, the region's focus on rural and eco-tourism suggests that some sections or shorter, less technical paths might be suitable for families. However, specific 'easy' mountain bike routes are not predominantly featured in the current selection, so careful route planning is advised.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Abrucena?
A: The Sierra Nevada region, including Abrucena, offers diverse conditions. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant weather for mountain biking, avoiding the intense summer heat and potential winter snow at higher elevations. The varied ecosystems mean you can find suitable conditions across different seasons depending on the altitude.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Abrucena?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular. For example, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013394058/transnevada-and-encinar-de-abrucena-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Transnevada and Encinar de Abrucena Loop&lt;/a&gt; covers 41.5 km, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10303779" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abrucena – Abrucena Cemetery loop from Abrucena&lt;/a&gt; is another popular 41.2 km circular option.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on the trails?
A: Mountain biking in Abrucena offers stunning natural scenery within the Sierra Nevada Natural Park. You'll encounter diverse ecosystems, from pine slopes and ravines to rocky and arid sections. The region is also known for crystal-clear waterfalls, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5590550" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Small Waterfall of the Andarax River&lt;/a&gt; and another &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5430550" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, and significant peaks like Cerro del Rayo and Buitre peak.

Q: Are there any specific regulations for mountain biking in the Sierra Nevada Natural Park?
A: As Abrucena is located within the Sierra Nevada Natural Park, it's important to be aware of any specific regulations regarding trail use, environmental protection, and permits. While general rules apply, it's always advisable to check local park authority guidelines before your ride to ensure compliance and protect the natural environment.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Abrucena?
A: The mountain bike trails around Abrucena are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. Riders often praise the challenging ascents and exhilarating descents, the diverse terrain including natural rock gardens, and the stunning mountainous landscapes of the Sierra Nevada Natural Park.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or attractions near the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, beyond the trails, you can explore charming villages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3291591" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almócita&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5303835" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abla Village&lt;/a&gt;, which showcase Moorish heritage. Natural highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5144331" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chullo Peak&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4457126" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abrucena Holm Oak Forest&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter interesting structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4001271" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge over the Chillo Ravine&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is there parking available for mountain bikers in Abrucena?
A: Abrucena is a small village, and while specific mountain bike parking areas are not detailed, parking is generally available within or on the outskirts of the town. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival for the most convenient and permitted parking spots, especially if you plan to start your ride directly from the village center.

Q: Are there any trails that pass through the Abrucena Holm Oak Forest?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1190679273/abrucena-holm-oak-forest-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abrucena Holm Oak Forest Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 24.7 km route that specifically winds through sections of the holm oak forest, offering a distinct natural environment for your ride.

Q: Can I find mountain bike routes that start from Fiñana?
A: Yes, there are routes that start from nearby towns. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40003915" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fiñana – Doctor's bivouac shelter loop from Fiñana&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 65.1 km route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10303780" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abrucena Cemetery – Abla loop from Fiñana&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 46.5 km option, both offering different starting points for exploration.
tips: This holm oak forest is included in the Network of Protected Natural Areas of Andalusia, with the figures of Sierra Nevada Natural and National Park. It can be visited in spring, summer and autumn.
This waterfall is very close to the road. It does not usually have water all seasons of the year.

faqs.question: Is the Transnevada and Encinar de Abrucena Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this mountain bike loop is considered difficult and is best suited for experienced riders. It requires strong endurance, advanced riding skills, and you should be prepared for significant climbs, chunky rock descents, and potential hike-a-bike sections.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain is highly diverse and challenging. You can expect a 'gnarly web of doubletrack, single track, gravel, and footpaths'. Approximately 99% of the route is unpaved dirt roads and tracks, with some paved sections where no other paths are available. Be ready for chunky rock descents and steep climbs.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks can I expect to see on the route?
faqs.answer: The loop offers stunning views of the Sierra Nevada National Park, traversing alpine terrain between 1,500 and 2,000 meters above sea level. You'll pass through the beautiful Abrucena Holm Oak Forest and encounter a scenic Waterfall. The region is rich in biodiversity, with wildflower-covered peaks and traditional mountain villages.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this loop?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are considered ideal for riding this route. During these seasons, the weather is generally more stable, temperatures are moderate, and you can enjoy blooming wildflowers and vibrant autumn colors.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for this loop?
faqs.answer: The loop starts and ends in Abrucena. You can typically find parking within the village itself, which serves as the primary access point for this trail.

faqs.question: Is the Transnevada and Encinar de Abrucena Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Sierra Nevada National Park generally allows dogs, it's crucial to keep them on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure their safety, especially given the challenging terrain and potential for encountering other trail users or livestock. Always practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: As this loop is located within or near the Sierra Nevada National Park, there are generally no specific entrance fees for accessing the park's trails for activities like mountain biking. However, certain activities or protected zones might require permits, so it's always wise to check the official Sierra Nevada National Park website for the latest regulations before your visit.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other trails. You'll ride along parts of the Encinar de Abrucena, the Transnevada (Stage 3 / 8), and the Transnevada Ruta C. It also briefly touches on Camino de las Moriscas - Castillejo, La Jairola - El Castillejo, Abla - La Jairola, Carretra la Roza, and Vereda Alta.

faqs.question: What should I pack for mountain biking this challenging loop?
faqs.answer: Given its difficulty and length, you should pack essentials for a demanding mountain bike ride. This includes plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike, appropriate layers of clothing for changing mountain weather, sun protection, a first-aid kit, and a reliable navigation device with the route downloaded. Due to potential hike-a-bike sections, comfortable and sturdy cycling shoes are also recommended.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the loop starts and ends in Abrucena, you can find local amenities there. Traditional mountain villages in the region, like Abrucena, often offer cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options, allowing for an authentic connection to Andalusian rural life and a chance to refuel after your ride.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike loop?
faqs.answer: This loop is approximately 41.5 km (25.8 miles) long with over 1000 meters of elevation gain. For an experienced mountain biker, it typically takes around 4 hours and 8 minutes to complete, but this can vary significantly based on fitness levels, technical skill, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.
